step_by_step_thinking: I concur with the consensus among agents. When the soldering torch temperature abruptly increases, it triggers internal stress generation and initiates phase transformations that result in higher yield strength while reducing plasticity (decreasing percent elongation). Therefore, my final answer remains consistent with option C: "Yield strength increases and percent elongation decreases." This outcome reflects the typical metallurgical responses seen upon subjecting materials to non-uniform thermal treatments. Thus, maintaining controlled temperatures is crucial for ensuring proper mechanical properties in dental restorations using cobalt-chromium alloys. Final
